cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what the father did when he was laid off. The options are A to D. The passage describes the situation when the father was laid off. The father came home early, and when the narrator, Andrew, asked why, the father answered quietly, "Andrew, I was laid off today." Then the narrator thought he was joking at first but realized he was telling the truth. The passage mentions that the father's expression showed the truth. The father was a hard worker and proud of his career, so being laid off affected him. The story shows that his unemployment caused changes in their lives and that the father was down and trying to be optimistic. He even started his own business after months of searching. Looking at the options: A. was angry with his boss – The passage doesn't mention anything about the father being angry with his boss. He just says he was laid off. B. didn't care about it at all – That's the opposite of what's described. The father was clearly affected. C. couldn't accept the fact easily – The father's reaction included being quiet, and the narrator noticing his down expression. He also spent time searching for jobs and eventually took a new direction. This suggests difficulty accepting it at first. D. was as happy as usual – No, the father wasn't happy. He was affected and down. So the answer should be C. The father couldn't
